questTitle: Secondary 3 E-Math: Standard Form and Applications Worksheet 2
subject: Mathematics
description: Master standard form (scientific notation)! This worksheet covers converting between standard form and ordinary numbers, working with metric prefixes (mega, kilo, milli, micro, nano), performing calculations in standard form, and applying standard form to real-world problems. Show all working clearly!

questions.question: A number in standard form is written as A × 10ⁿ. What are the conditions for A and n?
options: A must be a whole number, n is any integer
A can be any number, n must be positive

questions.question: Which of the following is correctly written in standard form?
What is the value of 10⁶?
What is the value of 10⁻³?
Express 45,000,000 in standard form:
Express 0.00034 in standard form:
Express 0.000000507 in standard form:
Express 8,230,000,000 in standard form:
Convert 3.56 × 10⁵ to an ordinary number:
Convert 7.2 × 10⁻⁴ to an ordinary number:

Match the metric prefixes with their values:

• kilo (k) = 10³ = 1,000
• mega (M) = 10⁶ = 1,000,000
• giga (G) = 10⁹ = 1,000,000,000

Which row shows the correct values?

Match the metric prefixes with their values:

• milli (m) = 10⁻³ = 0.001
• micro (μ) = 10⁻⁶ = 0.000001
• nano (n) = 10⁻⁹ = 0.000000001

Which row shows the correct values?

Convert 5.2 km to metres and express in standard form.

Note: 1 km (kilo = 10³) = 1000 m

5.2 km = 5.2 × 1000 m = 5200 m

Convert 45 mg to grams and express in standard form.

Note: 1 mg (milli = 10⁻³) = 0.001 g

45 mg = 45 × 10⁻³ g = 0.045 g

Convert 3.5 GHz to Hz and express in standard form.

Note: 1 GHz (giga = 10⁹) = 1,000,000,000 Hz

3.5 GHz = 3.5 × 10⁹ Hz

A computer chip has a transistor width of 7 nanometres. Express this in metres in standard form.

Note: 1 nm (nano = 10⁻⁹) = 0.000000001 m

7 nm = 7 × 10⁻⁹ m

A bacteria cell has a length of 2.5 micrometres. Express this in metres in standard form.

Note: 1 μm (micro = 10⁻⁶) = 0.000001 m

2.5 μm = 2.5 × 10⁻⁶ m

Calculate: (3 × 10⁴) × (2 × 10⁵)

Step 1: 3 × 2 = 6
Step 2: 10⁴ × 10⁵ = 10⁹
Answer: 6 × 10⁹

Calculate: (4 × 10⁻³) × (5 × 10⁷)

Give your answer in standard form.

Calculate: (8 × 10⁵) × (7 × 10³)

Step 1: 8 × 7 = 56
Step 2: 10⁵ × 10³ = 10⁸
Step 3: 56 × 10⁸ = 5.6 × 10⁹ (adjust to standard form)

Calculate: (9 × 10⁸) ÷ (3 × 10⁵)

Step 1: 9 ÷ 3 = 3
Step 2: 10⁸ ÷ 10⁵ = 10³

Calculate: (6.4 × 10⁻²) ÷ (8 × 10⁴)

Give your answer in standard form.

Calculate: (3.5 × 10⁴) + (2.3 × 10⁴)

Since powers of 10 are the same, add the coefficients:
(3.5 + 2.3) × 10⁴

Calculate: (5.2 × 10⁶) + (3.8 × 10⁵)

First, express both with the same power of 10:
5.2 × 10⁶ = 52 × 10⁵
Then: (52 + 3.8) × 10⁵ = 55.8 × 10⁵ = 5.58 × 10⁶

The distance from Earth to the Sun is approximately 150 million kilometres. Express this distance in metres in standard form.

Note: 1 million = 10⁶

150 million km = 150 × 10⁶ km
= 150 × 10⁶ × 10³ m (since 1 km = 10³ m)
= 150 × 10⁹ m = 1.5 × 10¹¹ m

The population of China is approximately 1.4 billion people. Express this in standard form.

Note: 1 billion = 10⁹ = 1,000,000,000

1.4 billion = 1.4 × 10⁹

A grain of sand has a mass of approximately 50 micrograms. Express this in grams in standard form.

Note: 1 μg (micro = 10⁻⁶) = 0.000001 g

50 μg = 50 × 10⁻⁶ g = 5 × 10⁻⁵ g

A computer can perform 3.5 trillion calculations per second. Express this in standard form.

Note: 1 trillion = 10¹² = 1,000,000,000,000

3.5 trillion = 3.5 × 10¹²

Light travels at approximately 3 × 10⁸ metres per second. How far does light travel in 1 minute?

1 minute = 60 seconds
Distance = 3 × 10⁸ × 60 = 180 × 10⁸ m = 1.8 × 10¹⁰ m

A hard drive has a capacity of 2 terabytes. Express the capacity in bytes in standard form.

Note: 1 terabyte (tera = 10¹²) = 10¹² bytes

2 terabytes = 2 × 10¹² bytes

A typical virus has a diameter of 100 nanometres. How many viruses placed side by side would fit across a width of 1 millimetre?

Note: 1 nm (nano = 10⁻⁹) = 10⁻⁹ m
      1 mm (milli = 10⁻³) = 10⁻³ m

1 mm = 10⁻³ m = 10⁶ nm
100 nm = 10² nm
Number = 10⁶ ÷ 10² = 10⁴

The mass of an electron is 9.1 × 10⁻³¹ kg. The mass of a proton is 1.67 × 10⁻²⁷ kg. How many times heavier is a proton than an electron?

Ratio = (1.67 × 10⁻²⁷) ÷ (9.1 × 10⁻³¹)
= (1.67 ÷ 9.1) × 10⁻²⁷⁺³¹
= 0.184 × 10⁴
= 1.84 × 10³ ≈ 1836

A water droplet has a volume of 0.05 mL. Express this in litres in standard form.

Note: 1 mL (milli = 10⁻³) = 10⁻³ L = 0.001 L

0.05 mL = 0.05 × 10⁻³ L = 5 × 10⁻² × 10⁻³ L = 5 × 10⁻⁵ L

Singapore's GDP is approximately $400 billion. The population is approximately 5.5 million. Calculate the GDP per capita in standard form.

Note: 1 billion = 10⁹, 1 million = 10⁶

GDP per capita = (4 × 10¹¹) ÷ (5.5 × 10⁶)
= (4 ÷ 5.5) × 10⁵
≈ 0.727 × 10⁵
= 7.27 × 10⁴

The thickness of a human hair is about 70 micrometres. Express this in millimetres.

Note: 1 μm (micro = 10⁻⁶) = 10⁻⁶ m
      1 mm (milli = 10⁻³) = 10⁻³ m

70 μm = 70 × 10⁻⁶ m = 7 × 10⁻⁵ m
In mm: 7 × 10⁻⁵ m ÷ 10⁻³ = 7 × 10⁻² mm

A country produces 2.5 million barrels of oil per day. How many barrels are produced in a year (365 days)? Express in standard form.

Note: 1 million = 10⁶

2.5 million × 365 = 2.5 × 10⁶ × 3.65 × 10²
= 9.125 × 10⁸

The wavelength of red light is 700 nanometres. The wavelength of blue light is 450 nanometres. What is the difference in wavelength? Express in metres in standard form.

Note: 1 nm (nano = 10⁻⁹) = 10⁻⁹ m

Difference = 700 - 450 = 250 nm = 250 × 10⁻⁹ m = 2.5 × 10⁻⁷ m

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Financial Applications with Large Numbers
questions.question:
A company's revenue is $3.2 billion and its expenses are $2.8 billion. Calculate the profit in standard form.

Note: 1 billion = 10⁹

Profit = 3.2 × 10⁹ - 2.8 × 10⁹ = 0.4 × 10⁹ = 4 × 10⁸

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Financial Applications with Large Numbers
questions.question:
A tech company is valued at $2.5 trillion. If there are 5 billion shares, what is the price per share?

Note: 1 trillion = 10¹², 1 billion = 10⁹

$2.5 trillion = $2.5 × 10¹² 
5 billion shares = 5 × 10⁹ shares
Price per share = (2.5 × 10¹²) ÷ (5 × 10⁹) = 0.5 × 10³ = $500

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Financial Applications with Large Numbers
questions.question:
A government budget is $85 billion. If 15% is allocated to education, how much is spent on education? Express in standard form.

Note: 1 billion = 10⁹

15% of $85 billion = 0.15 × 85 × 10⁹
= 12.75 × 10⁹ = 1.275 × 10¹⁰

The speed of sound in air is 340 m/s. A sound takes 4.5 × 10⁻² seconds to travel from source to listener. How far away is the source?

Distance = speed × time
= 340 × 4.5 × 10⁻² 
= 340 × 0.045
= 15.3 m

A bacterium divides every 20 minutes. Starting with 1 bacterium, how many bacteria will there be after 4 hours?

4 hours = 240 minutes = 12 divisions
Number = 2¹² = 4096 = 4.096 × 10³

An atom has a radius of 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰ m. A nucleus has a radius of 2.4 × 10⁻¹⁵ m. How many times larger is the atom than the nucleus?

Ratio = (1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰) ÷ (2.4 × 10⁻¹⁵)
= (1.2 ÷ 2.4) × 10⁻¹⁰⁺¹⁵
= 0.5 × 10⁵ = 5 × 10⁴

The Milky Way galaxy contains approximately 200 billion stars. If there are 2 × 10¹¹ galaxies in the observable universe and each has the same number of stars, estimate the total number of stars.

Note: 1 billion = 10⁹, so 200 billion = 2 × 10¹¹

(2 × 10¹¹) × (2 × 10¹¹) = 4 × 10²²

A medicine dosage is 0.25 mg per kg of body weight. A patient weighs 72 kg. Calculate the dosage in micrograms.

Note: 1 mg (milli = 10⁻³) = 10³ μg (since micro = 10⁻⁶)
      So 1 mg = 1000 μg

Dosage = 0.25 × 72 = 18 mg
18 mg = 18 × 10³ μg = 1.8 × 10⁴ μg

The Earth's circumference is approximately 4 × 10⁷ m. If a satellite orbits 400 km above Earth's surface, what is the circumference of its orbit? (Assume circular orbit)

Note: 1 km (kilo = 10³) = 10³ m

Additional radius = 400 km = 4 × 10⁵ m
Additional circumference = 2π × 4 × 10⁵ ≈ 2.5 × 10⁶ m
Total ≈ 4 × 10⁷ + 0.25 × 10⁷ ≈ 4.25 × 10⁷ m

A water tank holds 5000 litres. Water drains at 2.5 × 10² mL per second. How long does it take to empty the tank?

Note: 1 L = 10³ mL (since milli = 10⁻³, so 1 L = 1000 mL)

5000 L = 5000 × 10³ mL = 5 × 10⁶ mL
Time = (5 × 10⁶) ÷ (2.5 × 10²) = 2 × 10⁴ seconds

Convert to hours: 2 × 10⁴ ÷ 3600 ≈ 5.56 hours

Light from a distant star takes 4.2 years to reach Earth. If light travels at 3 × 10⁸ m/s, calculate the distance to the star in kilometres.

Note: 1 km (kilo = 10³) = 10³ m

Seconds in 4.2 years = 4.2 × 365 × 24 × 3600 ≈ 1.32 × 10⁸ s
Distance = 3 × 10⁸ × 1.32 × 10⁸ =
